bottlenecks: Dynamic-DeepHit has not yet been run under the same period-level protocol, so the external baseline comparison is still missing.
The final paper window set still needs to be narrowed so the main results table stays focused.
decisions: Use the method lineage period_ms -> period_ms_tree_query as the primary narrative in the paper and group report.
Keep landmark prefix expansion as part of the main experimental protocol while presenting risks at the period level.
findings: period_ms should be presented as an independent methodological contribution rather than only background for period_ms_tree_query.
period_ms_tree_query strengthens period_ms by letting static tree features participate in historical period selection.
The period token encoder in the current mainline is a KAN projector with shape 125->32->32 under the agro full-feature setting.
The tree-query attention is tree-conditioned additive attention driven by period tokens, lag embeddings, static summary, and interaction terms.
next_steps: Implement and run Dynamic-DeepHit on the same dataset organization and period-level evaluation protocol.
Prepare the method section and presentation materials so period_ms and period_ms_tree_query are described as a progression.
Finalize the primary evaluation windows for the paper after the external baseline is available.
summary: This session stabilized the method narrative: period_ms is the original period-level multiscale agricultural environment model, and period_ms_tree_query is its tree-specific attention enhancement. We also fixed the implementation-level wording for presentations: period features are encoded by a KAN projector into period tokens, and the tree-query module uses tree-conditioned additive attention rather than Transformer self-attention.
title: Clarified period_ms lineage and tree-query attention

decisions: Prioritize a clean matched-setting comparison before broader architecture changes: rerun period_ms, period_ms_tree_query, and period_ms_tree_query with spatial features under the same window set and optimization budget.
findings: The current period_ms versus period_ms_tree_query comparison is confounded by different training settings: period_ms_compare_windows_full5 used lr=0.001, batch_size=32, patience=20, while dynamic_hlb_tree_query_full5 used lr=0.0001, batch_size=128, patience=40, so architecture conclusions should not be based on that pair alone.

Under matched settings closer to period_ms_compare_windows_full5, the tree_query_spatial variant is promising on stable early windows, with 3->2 Ctd about 0.699 and 3->3 Ctd about 0.655, so static spatial structure should be prioritized in the next clean ablation.
next_steps: Run a matched-setting ablation on 3->2 and 6->3 first, then add Dynamic-DeepHit under the same repeated random hold-out protocol.
summary: This turn reviewed the current mainline and artifact summaries to prioritize next improvements. The strongest conclusion is that the current period_ms versus period_ms_tree_query performance comparison is not yet a clean architecture comparison because the runs used different optimization settings. We also found that the tree_query_spatial variant is a promising follow-up on early stable windows.
title: Identified clean-comparison gap and spatial tree-query follow-up

bottlenecks: trigger_orchard_v2 still has a calibration problem on longer horizons, especially 9->3, 3->9, and 6->6, even when its Ctd is strong.
The current CT auxiliary regression target is noisy and weak because its validation MAE is close to the naive baseline.
decisions: Keep the matched period_ms_tree_query comparison non-spatial; do not add tree-position features in this branch.
findings: In the matched non-spatial 5-repeat comparison on 6->1, 6->3, 9->1, 9->3, trigger_orchard_v2 reached mean Ctd about 0.655 versus period_ms_tree_query about 0.489 and original trigger_orchard about 0.436, while mean BStd worsened to about 0.482 versus about 0.296 and about 0.323.

The extra trigger_orchard_v2 window set 3->6, 3->9, 6->3, 6->6 preserved the strong-ranking / weak-calibration pattern: Ctd stayed competitive on 3->9 and 6->6, but BStd became very unstable on those long horizons.

Validation CT-delta MAE for trigger_orchard_v2 usually stayed around 3.7 to 4.4, which is close to the naive zero-change baseline on the same validation splits, so the current CT auxiliary regression is only a weak supervision signal.
next_steps: Tune trigger_orchard_v2 specifically for long-horizon calibration while checking whether its Ctd advantage over period_ms_tree_query survives.
Replace or simplify the CT auxiliary regression target, starting with direction-based or binned CT targets.
After the next trigger_orchard_v2 round, decide whether the paper should center the trigger_orchard_v2 branch or keep period_ms / period_ms_tree_query as the more stable main line.
summary: This session implemented trigger_orchard_v2 as a tree-conditioned continuous-window trigger-search model, ran matched 5-repeat experiments, and compared it against non-spatial period_ms_tree_query. trigger_orchard_v2 consistently improved Ctd over period_ms_tree_query and the original trigger_orchard, but its BStd remained unstable on longer horizons and the CT-delta auxiliary regression stayed near the naive baseline.
title: Stabilized trigger_orchard_v2 results and matched tree-query comparison
